message:
feat(coverage): add test coverage tracking and reporting (br-toy)

Implements comprehensive coverage tracking and reporting infrastructure:

Core Features:
- CoverageTracker: Thread-safe endpoint/branch coverage tracking
- CoverageConfig: Configurable thresholds (line/branch/endpoint)
- CoverageReport: Statistics and report generation

Report Formats:
- JSON output for CI integration with endpoint stats
- HTML report with visual coverage display
- SVG badge for README integration

Coverage Metrics:
- Per-endpoint hit tracking with status code breakdown
- Branch coverage hints for error path testing
- Threshold assertions (assert_threshold panics on violation)
- Untested endpoint/error path detection

Helper Utilities:
- record_branch! macro for branch coverage
- register_endpoints() for route table integration
- write_json/write_html/write_badge file output

Closes br-toy.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -277,7 +277,7 @@
 {"id":"fastapi_rust-syy","title":"HTTPS redirect and enforcement middleware","description":"Force HTTPS connections by redirecting HTTP requests and setting HSTS headers. Critical for production security.

**Implementation:**
- Redirect HTTP to HTTPS (301 or 307)
- HSTS header (Strict-Transport-Security)
- Configurable redirect behavior
- Proxy-aware (X-Forwarded-Proto)
- Exclude health check endpoints

**Configuration:**
- redirect: bool (enable/disable redirects)
- hsts_seconds: int (max-age value)
- include_subdomains: bool
- preload: bool (HSTS preload)
- exclude_paths: Vec<String>

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- HTTP requests redirected to HTTPS
- HSTS header set on HTTPS responses
- Health checks accessible over HTTP
- Proxy headers respected
- Configurable per-environment

**Tests:**
- HTTP GET redirects to HTTPS
- HTTPS GET returns HSTS header
- Health check accessible over HTTP
- X-Forwarded-Proto: https not redirected
- HSTS preload flag works","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:13:58.898720850Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-29T00:44:18.887776069Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T00:44:18.887638443Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["https","middleware","phase-2","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-syy","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-rhj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:33Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"fastapi_rust-t3v","title":"OPTIONS method auto-handling for CORS preflight","description":"Automatically handle OPTIONS requests for CORS preflight. When CORS middleware is enabled, generate correct preflight responses with Access-Control-Allow-Methods, Access-Control-Allow-Headers. For non-CORS OPTIONS, return 204 with Allow header listing supported methods for the route.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- OPTIONS requests handled before route handlers
- Allow header populated with route's methods
- CORS preflight generates correct headers
- OPTIONS disabled on routes where not wanted
- No body in OPTIONS response

**Tests:**
- OPTIONS returns Allow header with GET,POST for route supporting both
- CORS preflight returns Access-Control-Allow-Methods
- OPTIONS on non-existent route returns 404
- OPTIONS on method-restricted route returns only allowed methods","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:12:33.992024336Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:28:09.102856551Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T17:28:09.102718754Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cors","http","methods","routing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-t3v","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-4ck","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:33Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-t3v","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-bc8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:33Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"fastapi_rust-t4g","title":"Transfer-Encoding handling and hop-by-hop headers","description":"Properly handle Transfer-Encoding (chunked, gzip, etc.) and strip hop-by-hop headers per HTTP spec. Critical for correct proxying and body handling.

**Implementation:**
- Parse Transfer-Encoding header (comma-separated)
- Handle chunked encoding in body parser
- Strip hop-by-hop headers: Connection, Keep-Alive, Proxy-Authenticate, Proxy-Authorization, TE, Trailers, Transfer-Encoding, Upgrade
- Preserve only end-to-end headers in forwarded requests
- Content-Length vs Transfer-Encoding mutual exclusion

**Hop-by-Hop Headers to Strip:**
- Connection
- Keep-Alive
- Proxy-Authenticate
- Proxy-Authorization
- TE
- Trailers
- Transfer-Encoding
- Upgrade

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Chunked requests decoded correctly
- Hop-by-hop headers stripped when proxying
- Content-Length and Transfer-Encoding mutual exclusion enforced
- Trailers supported with chunked encoding

**Tests:**
- Chunked body decoded correctly
- Response with hop-by-hop headers stripped for proxy
- 400 if both Content-Length and Transfer-Encoding present
- Trailers accessible after chunked body","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:13:09.445341484Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:42:11.498818810Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T17:42:11.498752457Z","close_reason":"Fully implemented in crates/fastapi-http/src/body.rs: ChunkedReader for sync parsing, AsyncChunkedStream for async streaming. STANDARD_HOP_BY_HOP_HEADERS constant in connection.rs, strip_hop_by_hop_headers() function. Chunked encoding parsing with trailers support and extension handling.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["http","parser","phase-1"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-t4g","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-d6d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:33Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"fastapi_rust-u5j","title":"Response interceptors and transformers","description":"Allow post-processing of responses before sending. Useful for adding headers, transforming bodies, logging.

Implementation:
- ResponseInterceptor trait
- After-handler middleware hook
- Access to both request and response
- Can modify headers, status, body
- Chain multiple interceptors

Use Cases:
- Add timing headers (X-Response-Time)
- Transform error responses
- Add debug information
- Response body logging
- Content transformation

Acceptance Criteria:
- Interceptors run after handler
- Can modify all response parts
- Ordering is predictable
- Errors in interceptors handled
- Integration with middleware layer

Tests:
- Interceptor adds custom header
- Multiple interceptors chain correctly
- Body transformation works
- Error in interceptor doesnt break response
- Timing interceptor adds X-Response-Time","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:15:14.004114352Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T16:15:14.004114352Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["middleware","phase-2","response","transforms"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-u5j","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-rhj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:33Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"fastapi_rust-ucg","title":"NDJSON streaming response for large datasets","description":"Support Newline-Delimited JSON (NDJSON) streaming for returning large datasets incrementally. Essential for data export and real-time feeds.

Implementation:
- NdjsonResponse type
- Accepts Stream/Iterator of serializable items
- Sets Content-Type: application/x-ndjson
- Flushes after each line
- Memory-efficient (no buffering full response)
- Integration with asupersync streaming

Usage Pattern:
- Stream database query results
- Real-time log output
- Incremental data export

Acceptance Criteria:
- Each JSON object on its own line
- Proper Content-Type header
- Streaming starts before all data ready
- Memory stays constant regardless of data size
- Errors mid-stream handled gracefully

Tests:
- 1000 items stream without buffering
- Content-Type is application/x-ndjson
- Each line is valid JSON
- Memory usage stays flat
- Client can parse incrementally","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:15:11.030077812Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-29T01:31:56.138754977Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T01:31:56.138613934Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["json","phase-2","response","streaming"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-ucg","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-yme","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:33Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"fastapi_rust-uh6","title":"Content-Type inference and response helpers","description":"Implement response content type handling:
- Auto Content-Type for common response types
- JSON response helper with proper headers
- HTML response helper
- Plain text response helper
- Binary/octet-stream helper
- File download with Content-Disposition

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Json(data) sets application/json automatically
- Html(string) sets text/html; charset=utf-8
- File responses include proper headers
- No double Content-Type headers

TEST REQUIREMENTS:
- Unit: Each response type headers
- Unit: No header duplication
- Integration: Browser-compatible responses","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T14:39:41.340345959Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-28T00:39:38.682624317Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T00:39:38.682554948Z","close_reason":"Implemented Content-Type inference and response helpers: Added IntoResponse for Json<T> for automatic JSON serialization responses. Added Binary and BinaryWithType response types for binary data with automatic/custom content types. Added accessor methods for BinaryWithType. All tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["helpers","http","phase-1","response"]}
